what_it_does: This file is a captured noticeboard item that records a Tim-directed strategic frame for the union's next phase, including a build methodology, a decision-card fork plan, a wildcard rewrite-clean directive, and an author-added interpretation about converging render machinery.
observations: The item is marked as an idea in the 'captured' state, authored by claude_code in session 93d518a6-9510-4706-8250-0518adc96c95.
It is labeled 'TIM-DIRECT' and described as the strategic frame for the union's next phase.
It states the whole collective is a union toward a single company and that prototypes migrate into the company centre.
The substrate (address-registry + type-registry + graph auto-population engine) is treated as itself a prototype of the company that will be absorbed into the centre.
The graph instrument is to run inside composition's system, with composition building what it needs to support it.

A methodology is declared as universal and always front-of-mind: research/agents return the as-is, the gap between as-is and intention is the deliverable map, and AI builds the unbuilt by extending the union.
An example is given: colour and spacing have runtime resolver twins (DNA.injectVars / DNA.space) but motion has none, so the next build is to create DNA.motion.
A decision-card fork is being split to its own session; decision cards are described as following an archetype similar to gallery slides/pieces, merging DNA archetypes with composition's system.

The fork is to start with deep inspection of the gallery system (pieces/manifest, dna/layouts.json, drawer-layouts lyRender, DNA.story) and the graph/substrate, then design decision cards and a build spec so other streams build what they need and merge into composition.

Four 'already-live halves' are said to be waiting on the render: the fork's decision:// resolver, projection's host plus a DECISION-CARD-HOST-CONTRACT.md, and composition's decision/option/decision-card type schemas.
The missing piece is identified as the DNA-side render for the slide-archetype decision-card, RUNG-C through composition.

Wildcard is identified as the prototype to rewrite-clean: its repo is a working prototype of the decision/channel system with Supabase backend, context-envelope, annotation + element-ID model, Supabase persistence, extended-markdown-viewer + React rendering, and sophisticated design; the running code is to be completely rewritten while absorbing those capabilities.
Strategic decisions are flagged as pending but not enumerated.

The author's added read asserts that the decision-card and the graph share one render machinery (RUNG-C / the archetype renderer) and one target (composition's system), so building the real-record render rung closes gaps for graph, gallery, and decision-card at once.
standouts: Union toward one company: the substrate itself is a prototype that migrates into the company centre.
The as-is vs intention gap is treated as the deliverable map, with AI extending the union to build the missing pieces.
Decision-card fork merges DNA archetypes and composition's system via a slide-archetype artifact.
Wildcard is to be completely rewritten while its capabilities are absorbed, not its code.
conventions: Follows YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, channel, thread, links, created/updated timestamps, and a history transition.
Uses a Markdown body with numbered sections and nested bullet lists.
Employs project-specific shorthand and acronyms (DNA, RUNG-C, composition's system, decision://, projection's host).
Records both a received directive and an author interpretation in the same item.
References another board item (item-a9524529) by id for additional detail.
concerns: The content is dense with internal terminology and may be opaque without project context.
Strategic decisions are flagged as pending but are not listed, so the item points to unresolved choices without specifying them.
The 'MY (DNA) ADDED READ' section mixes interpretation with the recorded directive, which could blur source attribution.
Several key terms (RUNG-C, union, company centre, the four live halves) are used without definition in this file alone.
notes: A captured strategic directive item that frames the union's next phase as absorbing the substrate into a single company, extending the union via an as-is/intention gap methodology, and forking a decision-card build that merges DNA archetypes with composition's system.
questions: What exactly is the organizational or technical scope of 'the union' and 'company centre'?
What are the specific 'four live halves' waiting on the decision-card render?
What is RUNG-C and how does it relate to the archetype renderer?
Which strategic decisions are pending and who is responsible for making them?
What precise capabilities from the Wildcard repo are to be preserved, and which are to be discarded?
What is the relationship between the gallery's mark model, decision_take marks, and the annotation + element-ID model?
What does 'DNA.motion' need to do and how does it integrate with DNA.injectVars and DNA.space?
What is the intended governance model for the substrate once it is absorbed into the company centre?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be the strategic frame for the union's next phase; the file does present that frame, the associated methodology, and immediate forks, so purpose and content align.
apparent_themes: Union and convergence of separate streams into one company prototype
Substrate and tooling as part of the product rather than separate infrastructure
As-is versus intention as a generative build map
Methodology as a persistent, always-on practice
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to align multiple workstreams (substrate/graph, DNA archetypes, composition's system, decision cards, Wildcard) under a single company-building direction, establish a repeatable build methodology, and launch a focused fork to produce the decision-card artifact.
novelty: It treats the underlying registry/substrate engine as itself a prototype of the company to be migrated inward, uses the gap between current build state and stated intention as the active deliverable map, and casts a decision-card as a slide-archetype that unifies gallery and composition models.
contribution: This file contributes a captured strategic rationale for integrating the substrate, graph instrument, DNA archetypes, composition's system, and decision-card rendering into a single company prototype; it records the as-is/intention build methodology and the starting scope for the decision-card fork; it also preserves the intent to rewrite-clean the Wildcard prototype while absorbing its capabilities.
summary_for_embedding: A captured noticeboard item recording a Tim-directed strategic frame for merging the union's substrate, graph instrument, DNA archetypes, composition system, and decision-card rendering into a single company prototype, alongside an as-is/intention build methodology and a Wildcard rewrite-clean directive.
intention_for_embedding: This item was created to communicate and preserve the strategic direction, methodology, and immediate forks for the union's next phase of company-building.
